what_it_does: This file registers workshop document export behavior on window, generating standalone HTML, print-to-PDF markup, and deck-only PPTX files with motif-aware styling, bleed-aware colors, block rendering, and an export menu UI.
observations: Defines window.WSExport as the format dispatcher for html, pdf, and pptx exports.
Standalone HTML and PDF share buildStandaloneHtml; PDF opens a new window and invokes browser printing.
Motif CSS is embedded directly in this file for exported HTML output.
PPTX export dynamically loads PptxGenJS from jsdelivr when needed.
PPTX output maps motifs to solid slide background colors and adds simplified motif overlays for edgeband, watermark, and section.
Block rendering supports hero, headline, body, quote, icons, stats, palette, button, callout, image, and divider sections.
Export menu is a React component exposed as window.WSExportMenu and manages a local busy state.
standouts: A single document schema is rendered into both HTML/PDF markup and PPTX slide primitives.
Motif and bleed logic is applied across export targets, with richer CSS in HTML and simplified drawing in PPTX.
The file depends on several browser globals rather than local imports.
conventions: Uses window-scoped functions and registries for integration.
Uses inline CSS strings and inline React style objects instead of module-scoped stylesheets.
Uses optional chaining for global integrations such as dsaToast, WS_MOTIFS, CV_ICONS, and isLight.
Uses escapeHtml for most text interpolation in standalone HTML.
Uses format-specific switch statements over section kind values.
concerns: buildStandaloneHtml accepts opts but does not read it, so exportPdf passes print without a visible effect in this file.
Palette color values are interpolated into inline CSS without escapeHtml or validation.
Icon SVG markup from window.CV_ICONS is inserted into HTML without escaping, relying on that global data being trusted.
PPTX export uses remote script loading at export time, so pptx export depends on network/CDN availability.
PDF printing waits on a fixed timeout before focus and print, which may race slow font or layout loading.
Filename generation can produce a very short or empty base name if the title contains no matching characters.
notes: This file is the client-side export bridge from workshop documents to motif-aware HTML, print, and PowerPoint outputs.
questions: What shape and guarantees does the doc/page/section schema provide before reaching these exporters?
Are palette color strings and icon markup trusted inputs in this application?
Should opts.print change exported HTML behavior, or is it reserved for a removed path?
Is CDN loading acceptable for PPTX export in the intended deployment environment?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file contains real HTML, PDF, and PPTX exporters with motif and bleed handling, with the caveat that the print option passed to buildStandaloneHtml is not used directly.
intention_signals: It seems intended to let workshop documents leave the app as visually consistent artifacts while preserving motif identity across web, print, and editable presentation formats.
novelty: The distinctive piece is the paired export path where the same document blocks are translated into both standalone DOM/CSS and PptxGenJS slide instructions with shared motif and bleed decisions.
contribution: Provides a client-side export layer that serializes workshop documents into motif-aware standalone HTML/PDF and editable PPTX slides, including block-to-output translation, download orchestration, and an export menu surface.
summary_for_embedding: Client-side workshop exporter that converts motif-aware document pages and sections into standalone HTML, browser print PDF, and deck-only PPTX output with a React export menu.
intention_for_embedding: This was for taking ConceptV workshop documents out of the editor as portable branded artifacts while keeping motif, bleed, and section styling visible in exported formats.

symbols.does: Builds standalone HTML for a document and downloads it as an .html file.
symbols.description: This function calls buildStandaloneHtml, wraps the returned string in a text/html Blob, and passes it to triggerDownload. The filename is derived from the document title by replacing non alphanumeric or hyphen characters with hyphens and lowercasing it.

symbols.does: Builds standalone HTML for a document, opens it in a new window, and invokes the browser print dialog.
symbols.description: This function reuses buildStandaloneHtml with a print option, writes the generated HTML into a blank popup, and calls focus and print after a short timeout. If the popup is blocked, it reports that through window.dsaToast when available.

symbols.does: Resolves the motif id for a document page with a fallback to ivory.
symbols.description: This function delegates motif selection to window.WS_resolveMotif when that global exists. If no motif is returned, it uses the neutral ivory motif id.

symbols.does: Returns a motif bleed mode from window.WS_MOTIFS with a light fallback.
symbols.description: This helper reads the bleed value for a motif from a global motif registry. When the registry or motif entry is missing, it returns light.

symbols.does: Generates a complete standalone HTML document for all pages in a workshop document.
symbols.description: This function constructs CSS variables, export CSS, optional motif overlays, font links, and one frame per document page. Each page frame applies motif CSS, bleed classing, and section markup generated by blockToHtml, then wraps deck documents in a title and deck container.

symbols.does: Converts a section object into an HTML fragment for standalone export.
symbols.description: This switch-based renderer maps section kinds to specific markup patterns for hero, text, quote, icon, stat, palette, button, callout, image, and divider blocks. It escapes textual fields in most cases and consults global icon and lightness helpers for icon SVGs and palette label color.

symbols.does: Escapes basic HTML-sensitive characters in a string.
symbols.description: This utility coerces input to a string and replaces ampersands, angle brackets, and double quotes with HTML entities. It is used by the standalone HTML builders to reduce text interpolation risk.

symbols.does: Downloads a Blob by creating and clicking a temporary anchor element.
symbols.description: This helper creates an object URL, appends an anchor with a download filename, programmatically clicks it, then removes the anchor and revokes the object URL after a short delay.

symbols.does: Ensures PptxGenJS is available on window by loading it from a CDN if needed.
symbols.description: This async function returns an existing window.PptxGenJS when present. Otherwise it injects a script tag pointing at pptxgenjs 3.12.0 on jsdelivr and resolves after the script loads.

symbols.does: Exports deck documents as editable PowerPoint files with motif-aware slide styling.
symbols.description: This async function rejects non-deck documents with a toast, loads PptxGenJS, configures a wide deck, and creates one slide per page. It sets motif-derived backgrounds, bleed-aware text colors, simplified motif shapes, and renders supported section kinds as PPTX text, shapes, and layout primitives before writing the .pptx file.

symbols.does: Runs one export option from the export menu while managing busy state and errors.
symbols.description: This async inner function in WSExportMenu sets the active busy format, awaits window.WSExport for the selected format, reports failures through window.dsaToast, then clears busy state and closes the menu.
